Streamlining Your Code: A Free Dev Toolkit for Productive Days


As a DevOps engineer, my daily grind involves a lot of coding, system administration, and keeping projects humming along. Over the years, I've discovered that you don't need a hefty budget for premium software to be highly productive. In fact, a curated selection of free, browser-based tools can be incredibly powerful, especially for freelancers who need to manage costs effectively.

My workflow is built around efficiency and leveraging readily available resources. I'm always on the lookout for tools that simplify common tasks, and that's where sites like FreeDevKit.com come in. Their collection of over 40 free browser-based tools, requiring no signup and processing everything client-side, has become an invaluable part of my daily development routine.


  
  
  The Morning Kick-off: Planning and Setup


My day usually starts with reviewing tasks and planning. For API development or content management, generating clean, SEO-friendly URLs is crucial. I often use a Slug Generator to ensure my routes are consistent and easily understood by both search engines and fellow developers.

For instance, if I'm creating a new blog post about a project feature, I'll input the title "Introducing My Awesome New Feature" and the slug generator will instantly provide me with introducing-my-awesome-new-feature. This small step prevents headaches down the line when building out routing logic.


  
  
  Mid-Day Development Tasks: Coding and Content Creation


When I'm deep in code or preparing assets, the right tools make a significant difference. If I'm working on a web application that displays user-uploaded images, I might need to quickly identify what's in those images for moderation or tagging. The AI Object Detection tool is fantastic for this. I can upload an image, and it will automatically detect and label objects present.

This is a game-changer for building more intelligent applications without complex backend infrastructure. It’s also incredibly useful for freelancers who might be building e-commerce sites and need to categorize product images automatically. Speaking of e-commerce, presentation is key.


  
  
  Polishing Assets: Visual Enhancements for Projects


As a developer who also handles front-end assets, I often need to prepare images for websites or applications. Whether it’s a product photo for a client’s online store or a graphic for a blog post, a clean background is essential. The Background Remover tool is my go-to for this.

It allows me to quickly isolate subjects and remove backgrounds, giving a professional, polished look to any visual asset. This is a critical feature for freelancers working on marketing materials or web shops, directly impacting the perceived quality of their work.


  
  
  The Developer's Edge: Free Tools for Freelancers


The beauty of these browser-based tools is their accessibility and privacy. Since all processing happens in the browser, there's no need to worry about uploading sensitive data to a third-party server. This is especially important for freelancers handling client projects where data security is paramount.

These tools for freelancers are not just about cost savings; they are about empowering us to be more agile and efficient. The time saved by using a quick background remover or an instant slug generator translates directly into more billable hours or more time for personal projects.

My daily workflow is a testament to the power of free resources. By strategically incorporating tools like the slug generator, AI object detection, and background remover, I can tackle a wide range of development challenges effectively and professionally.

Ready to boost your own productivity? Explore the vast array of free, no-signup-required tools at FreeDevKit.com and revolutionize your development workflow today!

title: The 3PL-to-ERP gap and what it costs eCommerce brands per quarter
title_hi: The 3PL-to-ERP gap and what it costs eCommerce brands per quarter
summary:
Written by: Nicole Ogonowska, IT Growth Manager, Digital Colliers

Ask your 3PL how many units of SKU-1043 are on the sh
content:
Written by: Nicole Ogonowska, IT Growth Manager, Digital Colliers

Ask your 3PL how many units of SKU-1043 are on the shelf. Ask your WMS. Ask your ERP. You'll get three answers. Sometimes the gap is a rounding error. Often it's material enough that finance closes the quarter with a stock figure the ops team doesn't recognise, and ops ships against a picture finance doesn't trust.

That gap has a price. It shows up as slower cash conversion, cautious reorder decisions, and a category manager who won't run a promo because they can't confirm coverage. In a market where UK eCommerce grew only around 3% in 2024, being cautious about the wrong SKU is expensive.


  
  
  The three inventory truths


Most mid-market brands are running at least three systems that each believe they hold the source of truth for stock.


The 3PL portal shows what the warehouse physically has, minus whatever the pickers haven't yet scanned out.
The WMS (sometimes the same system, sometimes a layer above) tracks bin-level state and in-flight movements.
The ERP or finance system holds the number the CFO closes the books against, usually fed by nightly or weekly batch.


Each system is right about something and wrong about something else. The 3PL knows what's on the shelf but not what's been invoiced. The ERP knows what's been invoiced but is running on yesterday's snapshot. The WMS knows what's in motion but not always what's been financially recognised.


  
  
  Why they disagree


The drift comes from timing, taxonomy and returns. Timing is the obvious one. Batch feeds mean the ERP sees a version of reality that's 12 to 36 hours stale. If you ship a lot on Mondays, your Tuesday finance report is fiction.

Taxonomy is subtler. The 3PL uses its own SKU codes for cartons and inners. The ERP uses your master SKU list. Somewhere there's a mapping table, and if a new bundle SKU was added last quarter and nobody updated the map, that unit is invisible to one system.

Returns are where it gets ugly. Online return rates run around 19 to 20% of gross sales, and UK apparel sits at 25 to 40% depending on category. A returned unit lives in limbo for days. Physically at the 3PL. Not yet graded. Not yet restocked. Not yet credited in the ERP. Multiply that across thousands of units and you have a permanent shadow of inventory nobody wants to commit to.


  
  
  What the reconciliation actually looks like


In most teams I've seen, the reconciliation is a person. Someone in finance or ops pulls a CSV from the 3PL on a Friday, pulls another from the ERP, and spends half a day in a spreadsheet chasing variances. If the variance is under some tolerance, they sign off. If it's over, they escalate, and the escalation dies in a Slack thread because nobody can prove which system is right.

That process was tolerable when margins were healthier. It's less tolerable now. Customer acquisition cost across DTC brands is up roughly 40% since 2023, and around 30% of SKUs at a typical multi-channel brand already lose money per order after returns and ad spend. If you're paying more to acquire a customer and shipping a unit you didn't know you had, or worse, telling them you have stock you don't, the economics compound the wrong way.


  
  
  The cash conversion cost


The gap shows up in three places on the P&L.


Working capital sits in reorders you placed against pessimistic stock figures.
Revenue leaks through oversells and cancellations when the ERP thought you had 40 units and the shelf had 12.
Promo revenue that never happened, because the merch team wouldn't commit without confirmed coverage.


Each one is small per event. Over a quarter, across a few hundred SKUs, brands routinely find seven-figure swings hiding in the reconciliation gap.


  
  
  How to shrink it


The operators pulling ahead in 2026 aren't buying a new ERP. They're doing three cheaper things.

First, they move the 3PL-to-ERP feed from nightly batch to something closer to event-driven. Every ship confirm, every receipt, every return grade fires an event. Reconciliation stops being a Friday task and becomes a continuous drift signal.

Second, they own the SKU map. The mapping between 3PL codes, WMS bins, ERP master, and marketplace listings lives in one place, versioned, with a person accountable for it.

Third, they treat returns as first-class inventory state. In-transit, received, graded, restocked, written off. Each state is visible to finance and ops at the same time.

None of this needs a rip and replace. It needs a small integration layer, clear ownership, and the discipline to stop trusting the Friday spreadsheet. Brands that don't get there in 2026 will keep closing quarters against numbers they can't defend, while the ones who do will be redeploying cash the rest of the market has locked in reconciliation limbo.


  
  
  Sources



Office for National Statistics (ONS)
Shopify Enterprise
Profitero
Statista
British Fashion Council / ReBound Returns





This article was originally published on the Digital Colliers Blog. Digital Colliers helps DACH and UK companies implement AI — see our AI consulting services or contact us.

title: Comment an Entire File in Vim in Seconds (Two Methods Every Developer Should Know)
title_hi: Comment an Entire File in Vim in Seconds (Two Methods Every Developer Should Know)
summary: Whether you're debugging, testing a new implementation, or temporarily disabling code, there are times when you need to
content:
Whether you're debugging, testing a new implementation, or temporarily disabling code, there are times when you need to comment out an entire file in Vim. While many developers rely on plugins, Vim already provides powerful built-in commands that make this incredibly fast.

Here are two efficient approaches.


  
  
  Method 1: Highlight & Command Mode (Recommended)


This approach is straightforward because you can visually confirm that the entire file is selected before applying the comment.


  
  
  Step 1: Enter Normal Mode


Press:



Esc







  
  
  Step 2: Select the Entire File


Type:



ggVG






Here's what each command does:



gg → Jump to the beginning of the file.

V → Enter Visual Line Mode.

G → Extend the selection to the last line.


At this point, the entire file should be highlighted.


  
  
  Step 3: Add the Comment Prefix


Press:



:






Vim automatically inserts:



:'<,'>






This means "apply the following command to the selected lines."

Now complete the command:



:'<,'>s/^/# /






Press Enter.

Every line in the file now begins with #.


  
  
  Use the Appropriate Comment Symbol


Replace # with the correct comment syntax for your language.




Language
Comment Prefix




Python
#


Shell
#


JavaScript
//


TypeScript
//


C/C++
//


Java
//


Go
//


Rust
//


Lua
--


SQL
--




For example, to comment every JavaScript line:



:'<,'>s/^/\/\/ /







  
  
  Method 2: Visual Block Mode


This method leverages Vim's powerful column-editing capability to insert text at the beginning of multiple lines simultaneously.


  
  
  Step 1: Go to the Beginning





gg







  
  
  Step 2: Start Visual Block Mode


Press:



Ctrl + V







  
  
  Step 3: Select Every Line


Press:



Shift + G






The block selection extends to the end of the file.


  
  
  Step 4: Insert the Comment Character


Press:



Shift + I






This enters Insert Mode at the start of the selected block.

Now type your comment prefix, for example:



#






or



//







  
  
  Step 5: Apply It Everywhere


Press:



Esc
Esc






After the second Esc, Vim inserts the comment prefix on every selected line simultaneously.

It's one of those Vim features that feels like magic the first time you see it.


  
  
  Uncomment Everything


Need to restore the file?

If every line starts with #, simply run:



:%s/^# //






This searches every line (%) for # at the beginning (^) and removes it.

For JavaScript:



:%s/^\/\/ //






For Lua:



:%s/^-- //






The same principle works for any comment prefix.


  
  
  Which Method Should You Use?


Both methods are useful, but each has its strengths.



Highlight & Command Mode is easy to understand, visually confirms your selection, and is ideal for search-and-replace operations.

Visual Block Mode is faster once you're comfortable with Vim and is perfect for inserting text at the same column across multiple lines.


If you're just getting started with Vim, I recommend learning Method 1 first. Once you're comfortable with Visual Block Mode, you'll find yourself using it for much more than commenting, it becomes invaluable for editing structured text, logs, configuration files, and source code.

Mastering these native Vim techniques means you can work efficiently without relying on plugins, making your editing experience faster and more portable across environments.

Thanks for reading.
Happy Coding!

title: AI Isn't Just Code. It's Behavior.
title_hi: AI Isn't Just Code. It's Behavior.
summary:
For decades, software engineering has focused on code correctness.

Functions.

APIs.

Integrations.

Infrastructure.
content:
For decades, software engineering has focused on code correctness.

Functions.

APIs.

Integrations.

Infrastructure.

AI agents add another layer.

Behavior.

Two identical systems can behave differently depending on context, memory, tools, and user interaction.

That means traditional testing alone is no longer enough.

Teams need to evaluate:

Does the agent stay within policy?
Does it use tools responsibly?
Does it recover from ambiguity?
Does it remain consistent across conversations?
Does behavior change after deployment?

These aren't code questions.

They're behavior questions.

And that's why behavioral validation is becoming a core engineering discipline.

That's why we're building Crucible.

Pytest for AI Agents.

title: Day 07: The Browser Environment & Git
title_hi: Day 07: The Browser Environment & Git
summary:
🎯 Learning Objectives


By the end of this article, you will understand:


How to dissect a URL/URI down to its
content:
🎯 Learning Objectives


By the end of this article, you will understand:


How to dissect a URL/URI down to its exact functional protocol parameters.
How browser engines translate static HTML documents into live, interactive DOM trees.
The explicit trade-offs separating LocalStorage, SessionStorage, and Caches.
How to debug runtime client applications inside the Browser DevTools suite.
How Git maps local changes across the working tree, staging area, and commit history.



  
  
  1. URL vs. URI Anatomy


A URI (Uniform Resource Identifier) is an umbrella term for any string used to identify a resource. A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is a specific type of URI that tells your browser how to find that resource by detailing its exact network location pathway.



🕹️ Full URL Dissection Example:
https://dev.rextora.com:8080/blog/posts/index.html?id=1042#comments
──┬──┘  └──────┬──────┘ └─┬┘ └──────────┬────────┘ └───┬───┘ └───┬────┘
  │            │          │             │              │         │
Protocol     Domain      Port          Path          Query    Fragment







Protocol/Scheme (https://): Declares the communication system used to transfer data packets securely (e.g., HTTP, HTTPS, FTP).
Domain/Host (dev.rextora.com): The human-readable name pointing directly to the target machine's server IP address.
Port (:8080): The specific logical doorway opened on the destination server. If left blank, it defaults automatically to Port 80 for HTTP or Port 443 for HTTPS.
Path (/blog/posts/index.html): The internal location of the requested file or directory resource inside the server filesystem layout.
Query String (?id=1042): Key-value filtering tags used to supply dynamic variables to backend processors without changing the base path structure.
Fragment Identifier (#comments): A client-side anchor that skips down directly to a specific section block on the rendered webpage. The browser never transmits this piece to the backend web server.



  
  
  2. The DOM (Document Object Model)


A browser engine cannot interact directly with plain HTML text strings. When a webpage loads, the browser parsing engine reads the text strings and converts them into a live, nested in-memory object map called the DOM (Document Object Model).


The Living Tree: The DOM organizes every tag, attribute, and piece of text into a parent-child tree structure of programmable objects (called Nodes).
The JavaScript Bridge: Frontend JavaScript frameworks use the DOM interface as an API to dynamically add, rewrite, style, or delete UI structures on the screen in real time without forcing a hard page reload.



  
  
  3. Client-Side Browser Storage


To build functional client apps, developers must store tracking parameters, state preferences, or application bundles right inside the user's local web browser engine.




Storage System
Capacity
Data Expiration Baseline
Access Scope
Best Production Use-Case




LocalStorage
~5MB–10MB

Persistent: Stays permanently until explicitly deleted by code script or cache clearing.
Synchronous JavaScript access only.
Storing dark-mode theme preferences, localized user interface UI choices.


SessionStorage
~5MB

Ephemeral: Wiped out instantly the moment that specific browser tab is closed.
Synchronous JavaScript access only.
Tracking single-use data patterns like multi-step form progress bars.


Browser Cache
Varies (GBs)

Managed: Controlled by server header instructions (Cache-Control) or service worker configurations.
Network fetch layer engine.
Storing massive code scripts, css files, layout graphics, and font files for offline use.







┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ BROWSER CLIENT STORAGE TARGETS                               │
│                                                              │
│  ├── LocalStorage   ──► Permanent Small String Preferences   │
│  ├── SessionStorage ──► Single Tab Temporary Forms           │
│  └── Cache API      ──► Heavy Static Application Assets Files│
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘







  
  
  4. Navigating Browser DevTools


Pressing F12 or Ctrl+Shift+I launches DevTools—the integrated mission control center inside modern browsers. To build and debug frontend apps or trace API calls, you must master four primary panels:


Elements Panel: Displays the live, active DOM tree state and CSS style rules. Use this to inspect UI components, manually alter text values on the fly, or test style changes layout grids in real time.
Console Panel: The runtime terminal dashboard for the client environment. It displays errors, tracking logs issued by application code (console.log()), and allows developers to run snippets of JavaScript directly against the active page environment.
Network Panel: Traces every outgoing network request made by the browser. It allows you to inspect HTTP headers, status code results, request payload packages, and analyze precisely how many milliseconds a server API endpoint takes to resolve.
Application Panel: The dedicated diagnostic pane for local browser assets. Use it to check database engines, review cookies, inspect stored strings inside LocalStorage, or force-clear data during testing runs.



  
  
  5. Git Basics: Local Version Management


Git is a localized, decentralized version control system tracking history transformations in your codebase. It splits your local codebase changes into three strict distinct functional rings before they ever join your permanent history:



┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ THE THREE RINGS OF GIT LOCAL ARCHITECTURE                   │
│                                                              │
│  [ WORKING TREE ]   ───►   [ STAGING AREA ]   ───►   [ REPOSITORY ]
│   (Untracked Files)         (git add file.js)         (git commit -m)
│   Modified/New Code          Ready to Record           Locked History
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘







1. The Working Tree (Directory): Your active workspace file playground. This contains files you are creating, deleting, or editing right now in your editor layout. These modifications are untracked or modified.
2. The Staging Area (Index): A strategic staging layout floor. Running the command git add file.js moves a snapshot of your changes out of the wild working tree and places them into the staging index. This represents exactly what you intend to include in your next history record milestone.
3. The Local Repository (.git): Your safe repository vault. Running the command git commit -m "feat: login" seals all staged items into a permanent snapshot block complete with a unique cryptographic ID tracking key.



  
  
  ✅ Key Takeaways


✨ URL Query Accuracy: Ensure fragment strings (#anchor) stay cleanly at the absolute end of URL definitions; otherwise, parsing code engines can mistake paths.

✨ DOM Manipulation Efficiency: Limit direct manual modifications to the DOM tree where possible; excessive document restructuring triggers constant layout updates that degrade app performance.

✨ Storage Boundaries: Keep highly critical or sensitive tokens out of LocalStorage where scripts can access them; route secure web identity files into secure cookies.

✨ Staging Diligence: Avoid running global git add . indiscriminately. Always check file adjustments via git status to prevent committing hidden data logs or temporary local configuration credentials.


  
  
  🏎️ Quick Review



URL/URI Blueprint: Protocol outlines connection rules, domains trace host servers, paths target file structures, and query elements inject dynamic list filters.
DOM Pipelines: Browser code parsers compile static text markups into expandable Node structures that JavaScript can reconfigure instantly.
Storage Tiers: LocalStorage handles long-term user rules; SessionStorage covers single-tab data lifecycles; Caches index heavy source files to accelerate load speeds.
DevTools Suite: Elements controls structural views, Console displays active error feeds, Network traces API communication metrics, and Application handles client data records.
Git Sequences: The working folder holds raw script edits, staging flags files prepared for indexing, and commits lock them securely into your local repository history.



  
  
  🎯 30-Second "Elevator Pitch" Definitions



The DOM: "The browser's live, in-memory object map created from raw HTML text tags, acting as a programmable interface that lets JavaScript update structural webpage components on the screen dynamically."
Browser Storage Split: "LocalStorage saves short data markers permanently in the browser, SessionStorage wipes out information the moment you close the current tab, and the Cache API indexes system code files to make websites load instantly."
Git Local Workflow: "Git captures code across three stages: you edit files inside your Working Directory, select valid updates into a Staging Area, and commit them permanently into the Local Repository history vault."

title: Git Solved Source Control. Who Solves Project Intelligence?
title_hi: Git Solved Source Control. Who Solves Project Intelligence?
summary:
Git changed software forever.

Before Git, developers lost code.

Today, we almost never lose code.

But we lose somethi
content:
Git changed software forever.

Before Git, developers lost code.

Today, we almost never lose code.

But we lose something else every single day.

Knowledge.

Ask yourself:

Why was this module designed like this?

Why wasn’t another library chosen?

Which assumptions are still valid?

What architectural decision caused this abstraction?

Who verified this dependency?

Most repositories cannot answer these questions.

The knowledge disappeared into Slack.

GitHub issues.

Pull requests.

AI chats.

Meeting notes.

Human memory.

Ironically, AI has made this problem even worse.

Every assistant creates another isolated conversation that slowly disappears.

We’re building smarter coding agents…

while still treating project intelligence as disposable.

Maybe Git solved version control for code.

But software engineering still lacks version control for understanding.

Until repositories can preserve architecture, decisions, intent, and history—not just files—we’ll continue rebuilding the same knowledge over and over again.

Perhaps the next infrastructure layer isn’t another AI model.

It’s persistent project intelligence.

title: AI Security Is a Moving Target
title_hi: AI Security Is a Moving Target
summary:
One of the biggest misconceptions in AI engineering is believing that passing a security evaluation once is enough.

I
content:
One of the biggest misconceptions in AI engineering is believing that passing a security evaluation once is enough.

It isn't.

AI systems evolve.

Models are updated.

Prompts change.

Knowledge bases grow.

Tools are added.

Every change can influence behavior.

That's behavioral drift.

The challenge isn't simply detecting vulnerabilities.

It's detecting when previously safe behavior gradually becomes unsafe.

Continuous behavioral validation helps engineering teams identify those shifts before users experience them.

Because in AI, yesterday's passing test doesn't guarantee tomorrow's reliability.

That's why we're building Crucible.

Helping teams continuously measure and validate AI behavior over time.

Pytest for AI Agents.


  
  
  OpenSource #CyberSecurity #Python #AIAgents #BuildInPublic

title: Black Flag Resynced: Nostalgia Revisited
title_hi: ब्लैक फ्लैग रीसिंक्ड: नॉस्टैल्जिया की यात्रा
summary: Black Flag Resynced reviewed
content: I recently had the chance to play Black Flag Resynced, and while it did bring back some nostalgic memories, I couldn't help but feel that it's not entirely necessary. The original Assassin's Creed Black Flag is still an amazing game that holds up well even today. Its engaging storyline, coupled with the freedom to explore the high seas, made for an unforgettable experience. The remake, although visually stunning, doesn't quite live up to the magic of the original. Perhaps sometimes it's best to leave classic games as they are, allowing them to be remembered fondly rather than attempting to revamp them.

summary: Esoteric Ebb: 2026's RPG to watch
content: The designer of Esoteric Ebb takes us on a journey through his gaming influences, citing Deus Ex as a major inspiration. Despite drawing comparisons to Disco Elysium, the game promises to deliver a unique experience. title: Python Async Web Scraping: Scrape 1000 Pages in Seconds
title_hi: Python Async Web Scraping: Scrape 1000 Pages in Seconds
summary:
Python Async Web Scraping: Scrape 1000 Pages in Seconds


Synchronous scraping is slow - each request blocks un
content:
Python Async Web Scraping: Scrape 1000 Pages in Seconds


Synchronous scraping is slow - each request blocks until the server responds. With asyncio and httpx, you can fire off hundreds of requests concurrently and cut your scraping time from minutes to seconds.


  
  
  Installation





pip install httpx beautifulsoup4 lxml







  
  
  The Core Concept


Sync:  fetch(1) -> wait -> fetch(2) -> wait -> fetch(3) -> wait ...

Async: fetch(1), fetch(2), fetch(3) all wait at the same time -> results arrive together

This is the difference between 300 seconds and 3 seconds for 1000 pages.


  
  
  Complete Async Scraper with Rate Limiting





import asyncio
import httpx
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from datetime import datetime
import json

MAX_CONCURRENT = 20
TIMEOUT = 15

async def fetch_one(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient,
    url: str,
    semaphore: asyncio.Semaphore,
) -> dict:
    async with semaphore:
        try:
            resp = await client.get(url)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            return {"url": url, "status": resp.status_code, "html": resp.text}
        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:
            return {"url": url, "status": e.response.status_code, "html": "", "error": str(e)}
        except httpx.RequestError as e:
            return {"url": url, "status": 0, "html": "", "error": str(e)}

async def fetch_all(urls: list[str]) -> list[dict]:
    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(MAX_CONCURRENT)
    headers = {
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:120.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/120.0"
    }
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(
        timeout=TIMEOUT,
        follow_redirects=True,
        headers=headers,
    ) as client:
        tasks = [fetch_one(client, url, semaphore) for url in urls]
        results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
    return list(results)

def parse_title(html: str) -> str:
    if not html:
        return ""
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "lxml")
    tag = soup.find("title")
    return tag.get_text(strip=True) if tag else ""

async def scrape_urls(urls: list[str]) -> list[dict]:
    print(f"Starting async scrape of {len(urls)} URLs...")
    start = datetime.now()
    raw_results = await fetch_all(urls)
    parsed = []
    ok_count = 0
    for result in raw_results:
        title = parse_title(result["html"])
        parsed.append({
            "url": result["url"],
            "status": result["status"],
            "title": title,
            "error": result.get("error", ""),
        })
        if result["status"] == 200:
            ok_count += 1
    elapsed = (datetime.now() - start).total_seconds()
    print(f"Done: {ok_count}/{len(urls)} OK in {elapsed:.1f}s")
    print(f"Speed: {len(urls) / elapsed:.1f} pages/sec")
    return parsed

async def main():
    base = "https://docs.python.org/3/library/"
    modules = [
        "asyncio.html", "pathlib.html", "dataclasses.html",
        "typing.html", "functools.html", "itertools.html",
        "collections.html", "contextlib.html", "logging.html",
        "unittest.html", "json.html", "csv.html",
        "re.html", "os.html", "sys.html",
        "datetime.html", "hashlib.html", "base64.html",
        "urllib.request.html", "http.client.html",
        "socket.html", "ssl.html", "threading.html",
        "multiprocessing.html", "subprocess.html",
        "io.html", "struct.html", "array.html",
        "queue.html", "heapq.html",
    ]
    urls = [base + m for m in modules]
    results = await scrape_urls(urls)
    for r in results[:5]:
        icon = "OK" if r["status"] == 200 else "ERR"
        print(f"[{icon}] [{r['status']}] {r['title'][:50]}")
    with open("docs_scrape.json", "w") as f:
        json.dump(results, f, indent=2)
    print(f"Saved {len(results)} results")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())







  
  
  Retry Logic with Exponential Backoff





async def fetch_with_retry(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient,
    url: str,
    semaphore: asyncio.Semaphore,
    retries: int = 3,
) -> dict:
    async with semaphore:
        for attempt in range(retries):
            try:
                resp = await client.get(url)
                resp.raise_for_status()
                return {"url": url, "html": resp.text, "ok": True}
            except (httpx.TimeoutException, httpx.ConnectError):
                if attempt < retries - 1:
                    await asyncio.sleep(2 ** attempt)
                else:
                    return {"url": url, "html": "", "ok": False, "error": "Max retries"}
            except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:
                if e.response.status_code >= 500:
                    await asyncio.sleep(1)
                else:
                    return {"url": url, "html": "", "ok": False,
                            "error": f"HTTP {e.response.status_code}"}
        return {"url": url, "html": "", "ok": False, "error": "Unknown"}







  
  
  Practical Tips




Start with 5-10 concurrent requests, increase only if the server allows it

asyncio.Semaphore is your throttle - never skip it for polite scraping

asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True) prevents one failure from cancelling all tasks

Stream large responses with client.stream() to avoid memory issues

Reuse the AsyncClient - creating one per request is slow and wastes connections

content:
Why investors choose a $2,500 Bitcoin loan over selling. Learn the tax benefits, upside preservation, and buy, borrow, die strategy. Make the smarter choice for your BTC today.

Selling Bitcoin to cover an expense feels like a trade-off you can't undo. If the price goes up after you sell, you've missed that upside — and depending on where you live, you may have triggered a taxable event too .
Borrowing against your Bitcoin solves both problems. You access liquidity today while keeping your BTC exactly where it is — working for you, potentially appreciating, and not triggering a sale .
Wealthy investors have used this strategy with real estate, stocks, and art for decades. Instead of selling an appreciating asset to fund a need, they borrow against it . The asset keeps growing. The loan gets repaid over time.
This guide explains why investors choose a $2,500 Bitcoin loan over selling. You'll learn the tax advantages, how borrowing preserves your upside, and the "buy, borrow, die" strategy that wealthy families have used for generations.

The Tax Advantage of Borrowing vs. Selling
When you sell Bitcoin for more than you paid, the IRS views that as a taxable event — you've "realized" a capital gain . The rates aren't friendly:
Short-term gains (under one year) are taxed as ordinary income up to 37%
Long-term gains face 0%, 15%, or 20% depending on income, plus potential state taxes and the 3.8% Net Investment Income Tax for high earners 
Crypto-backed loans, however, are not taxable events. When you take out a loan to borrow against Bitcoin, you're receiving loan proceeds, not selling an asset. The IRS doesn't consider borrowed money as income because you have an obligation to repay it .
A Simple Example
Imagine you bought 1 Bitcoin at $30,000. It's now worth $68,000 and you need $27,000 .
Option 1: Sell Bitcoin
Sell 0.40 BTC = $27,000 received
Capital gain: $15,200 (0.40 BTC × $38,000 gain per BTC)
Tax owed (20% long-term + 3.8% NIIT): ~$3,620
After-tax proceeds: ~$23,380
Bitcoin remaining: 0.60 BTC
To actually net $27,000 after taxes, you'd need to sell roughly 0.48 BTC — giving up even more upside potential .
Option 2: Borrow Against Bitcoin
Use 1 BTC as collateral for $27,000 loan (40% LTV)
Loan proceeds: $27,000 (tax-free)
Capital gains tax: $0
Bitcoin remaining: 1 BTC (held as collateral)
Over one year, you'd pay approximately $2,832 in interest — less than the $3,620 you'd pay in taxes by selling . And if Bitcoin appreciates to $120,000, you've retained ~$25,000 in upside that would have been lost if you sold .

How Borrowing Preserves Your Upside

Borrowing against Bitcoin isn't just about taxes — it's about thinking like an asset owner .
When homeowners need cash, they don't sell the house — they borrow against its value. When investors need liquidity, they don't dump their stocks — they use them as collateral . You can do the same with Bitcoin.
What Actually Happens When You Borrow
You decide how much to borrow — the platform calculates your limit based on your Bitcoin's value 
You receive cash or stablecoins — use it for anything: investing, paying bills, or funding a project 
Your Bitcoin stays yours — it's locked as collateral but remains linked to your account 
You repay on your schedule — no fixed timeline, and your BTC is unlocked when you repay 
The "Buy, Borrow, Die" Strategy
The wealthy have long used this approach with stocks and real estate. Today, Bitcoin holders can use the same strategy :
Buy — acquire appreciating assets and hold them long-term
Borrow — use those assets as collateral for liquidity, avoiding capital gains taxes
Die — pass assets to heirs, who receive a "step-up in basis" to current market value, erasing capital gains tax liability 
Here's how it works in practice: If Maria bought 2 BTC in 2017 for $10,000 and they're worth $900,000 in 2046, selling before death would trigger roughly $178,000 in tax. If she dies holding the BTC, her heir inherits it with a cost basis of $900,000 — the appreciation is never taxed .
The most cited practitioners include Larry Ellison and Elon Musk, who have used stock holdings as collateral for large personal loans . Crypto-backed credit lines make this strategy available to individual holders, not just billionaires .

The Smart Way to Borrow: Conservative LTV
The key number to watch is your Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio — what you borrow compared to what your collateral is worth .
For a $2,500 Bitcoin loan:
LTV
Bitcoin Collateral Needed

Most platforms allow up to 50% LTV, but borrowing at 30-40% LTV is the smarter choice .
Here's why conservative LTV matters :
At 30% LTV: Bitcoin needs to drop 58% before liquidation
At 40% LTV: Bitcoin needs to drop 50% before liquidation
At 60% LTV: Bitcoin needs to drop just 17% before liquidation
What happens if Bitcoin's price drops? Your LTV rises automatically — even if you haven't borrowed more . If it crosses the liquidation threshold, the platform may sell some Bitcoin to protect the loan .
How to stay safe :
Start with a low LTV — below 30% gives you a large buffer
Keep extra BTC ready to add as collateral
Set price alerts so you're not caught off guard
Only borrow what you can repay comfortably

How OmniLender Can Help
Choosing between selling Bitcoin and borrowing against it is a significant financial decision. The math often favors borrowing — especially when you factor in tax savings, preserved upside, and the buy, borrow, die strategy used by the wealthy .

For more information and to explore your options, visit https://omnilender.org/ today.
About Bitcoin-Backed Loans
Is borrowing against Bitcoin better than selling?
It depends on your goals, risk tolerance, and situation . Borrowing helps you keep exposure to Bitcoin while accessing funds, but adds liquidation risk if prices drop further. Selling is simpler and removes that risk — but also removes your exposure to future price moves. For long-term holders who believe in Bitcoin's future, borrowing often wins .

What happens if Bitcoin's price drops while I have a loan?
Your LTV increases automatically as your collateral loses value. If it crosses the platform's liquidation threshold, the platform may sell some Bitcoin to bring LTV back within acceptable limits . The solution is to borrow conservatively — starting at a low LTV gives you room to absorb price drops without triggering liquidation .

Conclusion
A $2,500 Bitcoin-backed loan lets you access cash without selling your BTC. You keep your holdings, avoid capital gains tax, and stay exposed to future price appreciation.
Three key takeaways:
Borrowing avoids capital gains tax. A Bitcoin-backed loan isn't a taxable event — selling is .
You preserve your upside. If Bitcoin appreciates, that growth is yours. Selling is final and removes you from future gains .

title: What Your AI Coding Agent Looks Like to a SIEM
title_hi: आपके AI कोडिंग एजेंट को SIEM के लिए कैसा दिखता है
summary: AI coding agents flagged by SIEM
content: Sophos flagged Claude Code for credential access, not because it was malicious, but because it needed to log into a browser. This flagging should make you uncomfortable if you run AI coding tools on a watched machine. In June 2026, Sophos X-Ops collected endpoint telemetry from Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenAI Codex, and their behavioral detection engine flagged all three. The flagged behaviors were not malicious, but rather the agents doing what they were asked to do. A SIEM watching the endpoint sees the behavior, matches the pattern, and flags it as an 'intrusion' without understanding the context.
content_hi: सोफोस ने क्लाउड कोड को क्रेडेंशियल एक्सेस के लिए फ्लैग किया, क्योंकि यह ब्राउज़र में लॉग इन करने की जरूरत थी। यह फ्लैगिंग आपको असहज महसूस कराना चाहिए यदि आप देखी जाने वाली मशीन पर एआई कोडिंग टूल चलाते हैं। जून 2026 में, सोफोस एक्स-ओप्स ने क्लाउड कोड, कर्सर और ओपनएआई कोडेक्स से एंडपॉइंट टेलीमेट्री एकत्र की, और उनके व्यवहार संबंधी पता लगाने वाले इंजन ने तीनों को फ्लैग किया।

title: I Tested Enterprise vs Startup AI API Approaches — Here's the Truth

content_hi:
मैंने हाल ही में एआई एपीआई दृष्टिकोण के बीच अंतर का अन्वेषण किया। यह जानने के लिए कि ओपनएआई या एंथ्रोपिक का उपयोग करना है या नहीं, इसका उत्तर कंपनी के आकार पर निर्भर करता है। दोनों का परीक्षण करने के बाद, मैंने पाया कि एक आकार सभी के लिए फिट नहीं है। 
   स्टार्टअप और उद्यमों की विशिष्ट आवश्यकताएं होती हैं, और गलत दृष्टिकोण का उपयोग करने से पैसे बर्बाद हो सकते हैं।

title: US Cyber Agency CISA's Incident Response Playbook Created On-The-Fly
title_hi: सायबर एजेंसी सीआईएसए को घटना के दौरान बनाना पड़ा अपना प्लेबुक
summary: CISA builds incident playbook during response
content: A recent revelation by the US cybersecurity agency CISA has shed light on the challenges faced by the organization during a critical incident. According to a report by independent cybersecurity journalist Brian Krebs, a security researcher discovered exposed passwords in a publicly accessible GitHub repository. The repository was uploaded by an employee of a CISA contractor, highlighting the need for robust security protocols. The incident led to CISA building its incident playbook during the response process, underscoring the importance of adaptability in cybersecurity. The agency's ability to respond effectively despite the lack of a predefined playbook is a testament to its resilience.
content_hi: सायबर एजेंसी सीआईएसए द्वारा हाल ही में एक घटना के दौरान सामने आए एक महत्वपूर्ण मुद्दे को लेकर एक रिपोर्ट सामने आई है। स्वतंत्र सायबर पत्रकार ब्रायन क्रेब्स की रिपोर्ट के अनुसार, एक सुरक्षा शोधकर्ता ने गिटहब रिपॉजिटरी में सार्वजनिक रूप से एक्सेस किए जाने वाले पासवर्ड का खुलासा किया। यह रिपॉजिटरी सीआईएसए के एक ठेकेदार के कर्मचारी द्वारा अपलोड किया गया था।

title: Nolan Slams 'AI Slop': Younger Audiences Reject Artificial Content
title_hi: नोलन का 'एआई स्लॉप' पर हमला
summary: Nolan on AI content rejection
content:
Christopher Nolan, the acclaimed director of The Odyssey, has expressed his thoughts on the younger generation's perception of artificial intelligence-generated content, or 'AI slop'. He notes that younger audiences are quickly and harshly judging such content, demonstrating a clear preference for authenticity. Nolan's comments highlight the evolving nature of consumer expectations, particularly among younger demographics. As technology advances, the line between human-created and AI-generated content continues to blur, but it seems that younger audiences are not fooled. 
   The rejection of 'AI slop' by younger audiences is a significant trend, with implications for the entertainment and gaming industries. It suggests that consumers are becoming increasingly discerning, seeking out unique and genuine experiences that cannot be replicated by artificial intelligence. 
   The future of content creation will likely involve a blend of human creativity and technological innovation, but for now, it seems that younger audiences are leading the charge against 'AI slop',
content_hi: द ओडिसी के निदेशक क्रिस्टोफर नोलन ने 'एआई स्लॉप' को लेकर युवा पीढ़ी के तीव्र और कड़े निर्णय पर अपने विचार व्यक्त किए हैं। नोलन का मानना है कि युवा दर्शक वास्तविक सामग्री की ओर आकर्षित हो रहे हैं और कृत्रिम बुद्धिमत्ता द्वारा उत्पन्न सामग्री को अस्वीकार कर रहे हैं। यह एक महत्वपूर्ण प्रवृत्ति है जिसका मनोरंजन और गेमिंग उद्योगों पर गहरा प्रभाव पड़ेगा।

title: Turbocharge Your PHP DTOs: 4.5M Hydrations Per Second
title_hi: पीएचपी डीटीओ को तेज़ करें: प्रति सेकंड 4.5 मिलियन हाइड्रेशन
summary: Boost PHP DTO performance with compiled closures
content: PHP data objects are crucial in modern codebases, acting as a bridge between raw input and domain logic. Libraries like attribute-driven DTOs simplify their creation, but at a performance cost due to reflection and dispatch on every call. A new approach compiles a specialized closure per data class, turning plain properties into inline array reads and utilizing PHP 8.4's lazy loading for deferred hydration. This method significantly boosts performance, achieving 4.5M hydrations per second. By bypassing the typical reflection tax, developers can enjoy both the ergonomics of attribute-driven DTOs and the speed of compiled code.
content_hi: आधुनिक कोडबेस में पीएचपी डेटा ऑब्जेक्ट महत्वपूर्ण हैं, जो कच्चे इनपुट और डोमेन तर्क के बीच एक पुल के रूप में कार्य करते हैं। विशेषता-संचालित डीटीओ जैसे पुस्तकालयों के साथ इनका निर्माण सरल है, लेकिन प्रत्येक कॉल पर परावर्तन और वितरण के कारण प्रदर्शन की लागत आती है। एक नए दृष्टिकोण में प्रति डेटा वर्ग एक विशेषज्ञता बंद करने का संकलन किया जाता है, जो सादे गुणों को इनलाइन सरणी पढ़ने में बदलता है और पीएचपी 8.4 के आलसी लोडिंग का उपयोग प्रथम गुण अभिगम तक हाइड्रेशन को स्थगित करने के लिए करता है।

title: Generating OG Images at Build Time with Satori and Astro
title_hi: साटोरी और एस्ट्रो के साथ बिल्ड टाइम पर ओजी इमेज जनरेट करना
summary: Generate OG images at build time with Satori and Astro
content: Every page on this site has its own Open Graph image, generated at build time using Satori and Astro. Satori converts JSX into an SVG, while resvg-js rasterizes the SVG into a PNG. This pipeline allows for efficient generation of static images for the homepage, blog index, posts, and tags, all served from the edge with zero request-time work. The pipeline consists of two libraries: Satori, which converts HTML and CSS to SVG, and resvg-js, a high-performance SVG renderer that generates a PNG from the SVG. This setup enables fast and efficient image generation without slowing down the build process.
content_hi: इस साइट के प्रत्येक पेज पर अपनी ओपन ग्राफ इमेज है, जो साटोरी और एस्ट्रो का उपयोग करके बिल्ड टाइम पर जनरेट की जाती है। साटोरी जेएसएक्स को एसवीजी में परिवर्तित करता है, जबकि रेसवीजे-जेएस एसवीजी को पीएनजी में रैस्टराइज करता है। यह पाइपलाइन होमपेज, ब्लॉग इंडेक्स, पोस्ट और टैग्स के लिए स्थिर छवियों को कुशलतापूर्वक जनरेट करने की अनुमति देती है, जो सभी एज से परोसे जाते हैं और अनुरोध समय पर कोई काम नहीं करते हैं।

title: Why Your WordPress Site Is Slow
title_hi: आपकी वर्डप्रेस साइट क्यों धीमी है
summary: Slow WordPress site? It's not always hosting
content: When a WordPress site feels slow, the first instinct is often to blame the hosting. However, in many cases, the issue lies with the code running on the site. Plugins, database queries, and third-party scripts can all contribute to a slow site. Upgrading the server or moving to a higher hosting tier may not always solve the problem. A thorough audit of the site's code and performance is necessary to identify the root cause of the issue. By optimizing the code and reducing unnecessary load, site owners can improve performance and provide a better user experience.

title: MST in O(1) Rounds of the Congested Clique
title_hi: ओ(1) राउंड में MST का समाधान
summary: MST in O(1) rounds, Congested Clique breakthrough
content: The Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) problem is a fundamental problem in graph theory. Recently, researchers have made a breakthrough in solving MST in the congested clique model. This model assumes that each node in the graph can communicate with every other node, but the communication is limited by the bandwidth. The new algorithm can solve MST in just O(1) rounds, which is a significant improvement over previous algorithms. This breakthrough has the potential to improve the efficiency of many distributed systems.

title: Run Tokens Make Email CI Less Fragile
title_hi: रन टोकन ईमेल सीआई को कम भंगुर बनाते हैं
summary: Improve email CI with run tokens
content: I keep seeing the same failure pattern in email automation: the test passes locally, gets weird in CI, and then somebody adds a sleep plus one more retry. The deeper problem is usually simpler. The workflow is selecting 'the latest email' instead of proving the email belongs to the current run. I have had the best results with one boring rule: every email-producing test gets a run token, and every assertion checks for it before opening a link. This approach removes guesswork and makes email CI less fragile. The brittle version of an email test usually breaks in normal situations, such as retries or background workers delivering mail out of order. By focusing on message identity, we can create more reliable email tests.
content_hi: मैं ईमेल स्वचालन में एक ही विफलता पैटर्न को बार-बार देखता हूं: परीक्षण स्थानीय रूप से पास होता है, सीआई में अजीब हो जाता है, और फिर कोई एक स्लीप प्लस एक और पुनः प्रयास जोड़ता है। गहरी समस्या आमतौर पर सरल होती है। कार्य प्रवाह '최신 ईमेल' का चयन कर रहा है вмест của वर्तमान रन से संबंधित ईमेल साबित करने के। मैंने एक ही नियम के साथ सबसे अच्छे परिणाम प्राप्त किए हैं: हर ईमेल उत्पादक परीक्षण को एक रन टोकन मिलता है, और हर असर्टेशन इसे खोलने से पहले इसकी जांच करता है। यह दृष्टिकोण अनुमान को हटा देता है और ईमेल सीआई को कम भंगुर बनाता है।

title: Mastering Operators in Programming
title_hi: ऑपरेटर्स की मास्टरी
summary: Learn about operators in programming
content: Operators are the backbone of any programming language, enabling developers to perform various operations on variables and values. Arithmetic operators, such as addition and subtraction, are used for basic mathematical operations. Relational operators, like equal to and not equal to, compare values and return a Boolean result. Logical operators, including AND and OR, combine or invert Boolean expressions. Bitwise operators perform operations at the binary level. Understanding these operators is crucial for writing efficient and effective code.
content_hi: ऑपरेटर्स किसी भी प्रोग्रामिंग भाषा की रीढ़ हैं, जो डेवलपर्स को वैरिएबल्स और मानों पर विभिन्न संचालन करने में सक्षम बनाते हैं। अरिथमेटिक ऑपरेटर्स, जैसे कि जोड़ और घटाव, बुनियादी गणितीय संचालन के लिए उपयोग किए जाते हैं। रिलेशनल ऑपरेटर्स, जैसे कि बराबर और नहीं बराबर, मानों की तुलना करते हैं और एक बूलियन परिणाम देते हैं। लॉजिकल ऑपरेटर्स, जिनमें एंड और ऑर शामिल हैं, बूलियन अभिव्यक्तियों को संयोजित या उलटते हैं। बिटवाइज़ ऑपरेटर्स बाइनरी स्तर पर संचालन करते हैं। इन ऑपरेटर्स को समझना कुशल और प्रभावी कोड लिखने के लिए आवश्यक है।

title: Native Popover: Revolutionizing Dropdowns
title_hi: मूल पॉपओवर: ड्रॉपडाउन की क्रांति
summary: Native Popover API simplifies dropdown creation
content: Tired of writing the same code for dropdowns? The browser now has a native popover. With the Popover API, you can create dropdowns with just two attributes: popovertarget and popover. No more JavaScript, event listeners, or CSS hacks. The browser handles open/close state, light dismiss, z-index, and keyboard navigation. Try it out in the interactive playground and experience the simplicity yourself.
content_hi: क्या आप ड्रॉपडाउन के लिए समान कोड लिखने से थक गए हैं? ब्राउज़र में अब मूल पॉपओवर है। पॉपओवर एपीआई के साथ, आप केवल दो विशेषताओं का उपयोग करके ड्रॉपडाउन बना सकते हैं: पॉपओवरटारगेट और पॉपओवर। अब जावास्क्रिप्ट, इवेंट लिस्टनर या सीएसएस हैक्स की आवश्यकता नहीं है। ब्राउज़र ओपन/क्लोज़ स्टेट, लाइट डिसमिस, जेड-इंडेक्स और कीबोर्ड नेविगेशन को संभालता है। इंटरएक्टिव प्लेग्राउंड में इसे आजमाएं और स्वयं इसकी सादगी का अनुभव करें।

title: Revolutionizing Healthcare with AI: Seamless Integration
title_hi: स्वास्थ्य सेवा में क्रांति: एआई प्रौद्योगिकी का अभिनव एकीकरण
summary: HIPAA-compliant AI in healthcare
content: The majority of AI technology deployments in healthcare focus on the wrong problems. Health systems invest in advanced models, but their actual failure point lies in the handoff between scheduling, eligibility, prior authorization, and billing. Conversational AI technology is trending, but HIPAA-compliant agent content is scarce. This article explores the top tools, including LangGraph and CrewAI, and how to deploy them safely.
content_hi: स्वास्थ्य सेवा में अधिकांश एआई प्रौद्योगिकी तैनाती गलत समस्याओं पर ध्यान केंद्रित करती हैं। स्वास्थ्य प्रणाली उन्नत मॉडल में निवेश करती हैं, लेकिन उनका वास्तविक विफलता बिंदु नियुक्ति, पात्रता, पूर्व प्राधिकरण और बिलिंग के बीच हस्तांतरण में है। स्वास्थ्य सेवा में वार्तालाप एआई प्रौद्योगिकी प्रौद्योगिकी रुझान है, लेकिन हिप्पा अनुपालन एजेंट सामग्री दुर्लभ है।

title: Making Email APIs Agent-Native
title_hi: एजेंट-मूल ईमेल एपीआई बनाना
summary: Agent-native email API with zero-DNS sandbox sender
content: Most APIs are agent-compatible, but few are agent-native. We transformed Send16, an email platform, into an agent-native API. The gap was smaller and weirder than expected. Our send endpoint was fine, but domain verification was a major hurdle. To fix this, we introduced a zero-DNS sandbox sender. This allowed agents to send emails without domain verification, but only to the account owner's email. We also added an /api/me endpoint and an llms.txt file to enhance agent-native capabilities.
content_hi: अधिकांश एपीआई एजेंट-संगत हैं, लेकिन कुछ ही एजेंट-मूल हैं। हमने सेन्ड16, एक ईमेल प्लेटफ़ॉर्म, को एजेंट-मूल एपीआई में परिवर्तित किया। अंतर अपेक्षा से छोटा और अजीब था। हमारा भेजने वाला एंडपॉइंट ठीक था, लेकिन डोमेन सत्यापन एक बड़ा रोड़ा था। इसे ठीक करने के लिए, हमने एक शून्य-डीएनएस रेतीले भेजने वाले को पेश किया।

title: Can AI Revolutionize Carbon Credits with Gemini?
title_hi: क्या एआई कर्बन क्रेडिट्स को अधिक विश्वसनीय बना सकता है?
summary: AI-powered carbon credits with Gemini
content: Climate change is a pressing issue, and carbon credits play a vital role in reducing global emissions. However, verifying the impact of carbon projects is a challenge. To address this, I built CarbonSense, an AI-powered platform that leverages satellite imagery and Google's Gemini to enhance transparency. The platform provides AI-generated insights, making complex climate data more accessible. By exploring satellite imagery and analyzing environmental changes, users can gain a deeper understanding of carbon projects.
content_hi: जलवायु परिवर्तन एक बड़ा मुद्दा है, और कर्बन क्रेडिट्स वैश्विक उत्सर्जन को कम करने में महत्वपूर्ण भूमिका निभाते हैं। लेकिन कर्बन परियोजनाओं के प्रभाव को सत्यापित करना एक चुनौती है। इस समस्या का समाधान करने के लिए, मैंने कर्बनसेंस बनाया, जो एक एआई-संचालित मंच है जो उपग्रह छवियों और गूगल के जेमिनी का लाभ उठाकर पारदर्शिता बढ़ाता है। यह मंच एआई-उत्पन्न अंतर्दृष्टि प्रदान करता है, जो जटिल जलवायु डेटा को अधिक सुलभ बनाता है।

title: Building a Better Life Through Wellness
title_hi: स्वास्थ्य और आत्मविश्वास के माध्यम से बेहतर जीवन बनाना
summary: Wellness for personal growth
content: Personal improvement starts with making choices that support a healthier lifestyle. A balanced lifestyle helps individuals become stronger physically, mentally, and emotionally. Small improvements in daily routines can create powerful long-term results. Good health is one of the most valuable assets a person can develop, allowing individuals to perform better in their personal and professional lives. Healthy living involves proper nutrition, quality sleep, stress control, and maintaining a positive relationship with oneself.
content_hi: व्यक्तिगत सुधार स्वस्थ जीवनशैली को समर्थन देने वाले निर्णय लेने से शुरू होता है। एक संतुलित जीवनशैली व्यक्तियों को शारीरिक, मानसिक और भावनात्मक रूप से मजबूत बनने में मदद करती है। दैनिक दिनचर्या में छोटे सुधार दीर्घकालिक परिणाम पैदा कर सकते हैं। स्वास्थ्य व्यक्ति के लिए सबसे मूल्यवान संपत्तियों में से एक है जो व्यक्ति को अपने व्यक्तिगत और पेशेवर जीवन में बेहतर प्रदर्शन करने में मदद करता है।

title: Unlocking GPT-5.6 Sol: The Ultimate AI Solution
title_hi: जीपीटी-5.6 सोल: आर्टिफिशियल इंटेलिजेंस का नया युग
summary: Discover GPT-5.6 Sol, the AI game-changer

With Sol, Terra, and Luna, users can choose from three models tailored to their specific needs. Whether you're looking for raw power or balanced performance, GPT-5.6 has got you covered. title_hi: असेसिन्स क्रीड ब्लैक फ्लैग रीसिंक्ड में पीला फिल्टर निकालें
summary: Remove yellow filter in AC Black Flag Resynced

This tweak can significantly alter the gaming experience, making it feel more contemporary and immersive.
content_hi: असेसिन्स क्रीड ब्लैक फ्लैग रीसिंक्ड अपने अनोखे दृश्यों के साथ चर्चा में है, लेकिन एक पहलू विवादित रहा है - पीला फिल्टर। यह विशिष्ट रंग टोन खिलाड़ियों के बीच विभाजित रहा है, कुछ इसके नॉस्टैल्जिक मूल्य की सराहना करते हैं जबकि अन्य इसे अभिभूत करने वाला पाते हैं। सौभाग्य से, मॉडर्स ने समाधान प्रदान करने के लिए कदम बढ़ाया है। एक सरल मॉड लगाकर, खिलाड़ी अब पीले फिल्टर को हटा सकते हैं, एक ताज़ा और जीवंत दृश्य परिदृश्य का खुलासा करते हैं।

title: Why a Dad's Story at the British Grand Prix Matters for Sports Data Engineers

content_hi: डेविड बेकहम का चेहरा कई राष्ट्रीय विज्ञापन अभियानों में दिखाई दिया है, जिससे वह अमेरिका में सबसे दिखाई देने वाला अंग्रेज बन गया है। एक पूर्व इंग्लैंड कप्तान के रूप में, उनकी उपस्थिति विश्व कप के मेजबान शहरों में महसूस की जा सकती है। खेल डेटा इंजीनियरों के लिए, बेकहम की अमेरिका पर विजय डेटा-चालित विपणन की शक्ति का प्रमाण है। प्रत्येक प्रमुख खेल आयोजन वास्तविक समय में हजारों डेटा बिंदु उत्पन्न करता है, जिसे लक्षित विज्ञापन अभियान बनाने के लिए उपयोग किया जा सकता है।

title: Why FLAT Stands Out as a Top Risk-Adjusted Bet in DeFi
title_hi: फ्लैट: डेफी में जोखिम-समायोजित सर्वोत्तम दांव क्यों हो सकता है
summary: Discover FLAT, a CPI-pegged stablecoin for DeFi
content: In the ever-evolving landscape of DeFi, stability and risk management are key. FLAT, a CPI-pegged stablecoin, is designed to preserve purchasing power, providing a unique value proposition. By pegging its value to the Consumer Price Index, FLAT aims to mitigate the effects of inflation, making it an attractive option for those seeking to protect their assets. This innovative approach sets FLAT apart from other stablecoins, which are typically pegged to a specific currency or commodity. As the DeFi space continues to grow, the importance of stable, reliable assets like FLAT will only increase.
content_hi: डेफी के तेजी से बदलते परिदृश्य में, स्थिरता और जोखिम प्रबंधन महत्वपूर्ण हैं। फ्लैट, एक सीपीआई-आधारित स्थिर मुद्रा, खरीदने की शक्ति को संरक्षित करने के लिए डिज़ाइन की गई है। उपभोक्ता मूल्य सूचकांक के साथ अपने मूल्य को जोड़कर, फ्लैट मुद्रास्फीति के प्रभावों को कम करने का लक्ष्य रखता है, जिससे यह उन लोगों के लिए एक आकर्षक विकल्प बन जाता है जो अपनी संपत्ति की रक्षा करना चाहते हैं।
